page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating on an arm64 architecture, this server features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs with a single thread per core, running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and there are no integrated GPUs.

In benchmark testing, the c8gb.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Its memory subsystem is highly efficient, achieving top-tier results in both cached and uncached read-only memory bandwidth, as well as large-block write bandwidth. Single-core performance is moderate to strong, with Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25% and stress-ng single-core performance in the middle 50%. The instance also excels in specialized workloads, scoring in the top 10% for software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and database operations.

The instance features a high-capacity baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps, making it suitable for network-intensive operations. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARMv9 architecture and high physical core density deliver strong multi-threaded performance and high memory bandwidth without the overhead of hyperthreading. This resource profile is highly optimized for multi-threaded compute workloads, software compilation, static web serving, Redis databases, and medium-to-large scale LLM inference text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the 96-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. Running at 2.8 GHz on the arm64 architecture, it features 96 dedicated vCPUs with one thread per core, 192.0 GB of memory, and a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying on the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding throughput for database operations and software compilation. Single-core performance is moderate to strong. This instance is highly suited for multi-threaded compute workloads, static web serving, Redis deployments, and LLM inference text generation.

tagline: A high-density, 96-core ARMv9 compute instance delivering top-tier multi-threaded performance and 200 Gbps network bandwidth for demanding scale-out workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
Optimized for software compilation, Redis, and LLM inference
